label: Feature attribution & interpretability

node.label: Feature attribution & interpretability
node.definition: Interpretability methods attribute predictions or internal representations to input features, examples, concepts, directions, or learned components.
node.why_it_matters: The role of Feature attribution & interpretability is to connect Statistics & experimental design, Embeddings & distributed representations to downstream capabilities including Mechanistic interpretability & circuit analysis.
examples: substrate for Mechanistic interpretability & circuit analysis
limitations: Coverage is necessarily incomplete: measured proxies, policies, and test suites cannot enumerate every deployment context or adversary. Its direct dependencies include Statistics & experimental design, Embeddings & distributed representations.

Feature attribution & interpretability measures, constrains, audits, or governs behavior using Causal inference.
